slots.label: Subjective size perception conflict
slots.summary: Two reviews contradict each other on size: one calls the product 'too large', another 'very small'. Neither provides objective reference points, suggesting misalignment between user expectations and communicated sizing cues.

slots.label: Strengthen the product-page explanation and evidence around Missing size substantiation.
slots.summary: No objective size data (e.g., dimensions, weight) was retrieved from product text, yet size is the most frequently criticized aspect (frequency=2) and fully negative (negative_ratio=1.0). Adding explicit, standardized size specifications would enable users to calibrate expectations and reduce contradictory size complaints.; The Korean product title contains no size or aesthetic descriptors, limiting its utility for cross-cultural or search-based validation.

slots.label: Strengthen the product-page explanation and evidence around Positive design perception.
slots.summary: A reviewer used 'beautiful' to describe design, but no matching aesthetic language (e.g., 'elegant', 'modern') was found in the retrieved product title. This suggests a gap between user-perceived qualities and marketing language, potentially weakening resonance.; Only the product title was retrieved — richer descriptive copy (e.g., bullet points, feature highlights) may contain relevant design language that was not accessed.
